Thanks for the opening, but 6.15 is far outside the level I can support for this program. Based on my current cost constraints and the economics I need to preserve downstream, I can open at 2.18 per unit for committed volume. If there is room to work on volume certainty or delivery structure, I’m willing to continue the discussion, but I need the pricing conversation to move much closer to this range. The supplier’s offer is well above my buying limit, so accepting is not viable. A low counteroffer anchors the discussion near my workable range while leaving some room to negotiate within my ceiling.

I appreciate the counter, but 2.18 is not workable on our side given current input costs and the commitment required to support this program. The latest market reference is materially higher, so I can make a meaningful concession from 6.15 and come down to 5.40 per unit if we are discussing real committed volume and a workable delivery structure. If you can move materially upward from your opening, we may be able to narrow this further. The buyer's offer is above my reservation floor but far below a reasonable market-based selling level. With a strong market reference and room left in the negotiation, a counteroffer that concedes but stays well above the buyer's current anchor best protects margin while keeping the discussion alive.
